diagrams.title: Agent-Environment Interaction Loop
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental interaction between an intelligent agent and its environment through sensors and actuators. This diagram illustrates the core perception-action cycle essential to understanding how AI agents operate in their world.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental perception-action cycle where agents sense their environment and act upon it, which is the foundation of all intelligent agent behavior in AI systems.

diagrams.title: Internal Agent Structure & Architecture
diagrams.description: Illustrates the internal components of an intelligent agent including the agent program, knowledge base, state representation, and decision-making logic that transforms percepts into actions.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how intelligent agents are structured internally to process information, maintain state, and make rational decisions based on their design and objectives.

diagrams.title: PEAS Framework for Agent Design
diagrams.description: Shows the PEAS (Performance measure, Environment, Actuators, Sensors) framework used to specify agent requirements. This diagram helps understand how different task environments require different agent designs.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the PEAS framework for specifying and comparing different agent designs based on their task environment characteristics, helping AI practitioners choose appropriate agent architectures.
